title: Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Punjab

overview.short: Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the foothills of the Shivalik Hills in Rupnagar district, in the state of Punjab, India.
overview.description: Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in the foothills of the Shivalik Hills in Rupnagar district, in the state of Punjab, India. It spans an area of 116 hectares and forms a part of the larger Nangal Wetland, situated on the banks of the Sutlej River. The sanctuary is a significant ecological habitat supporting a range of flora and fauna, including several threatened species. It is a section of a man-made reservoir on the Sutlej River that was created in 1961 as part of the Bhakra-Nangal Project.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Nangal
topPlaces.description: Nangal is a town, near city of Rupnagar in Rupnagar district in Punjab, India. It sits at the foot of the Shiwalik Hills where it was established after plans for a dam required the movement of previously established villages.

topPlaces.name: Mehatpur Basdehra
topPlaces.description: Mehatpur Basdehra is a town and a Nagar Parishad Sub Tehsil in the Una District of the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Dehlan, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Dehlan Lower, is a village located in Una district of the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Recently, Dehlan was adopted by Shri Anurag Singh Thakur, the sitting Member of Parliament from the Hamirpur seat under Sansad Adarsh Gram Yojna.

topPlaces.name: Santokhgarh
topPlaces.description: Santokhgarh is a town and a Municipal Council, nagar panchayat in Una district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Bhakra Dam
topPlaces.description: Bhakra Nangal Dam is a concrete gravity dam on the Satluj River in Bhakra Village in Bilaspur district, Himachal Pradesh in northern India. The dam forms the Gobind Sagar reservoir.

topPlaces.name: Jawaharlal Navoday Vidhyalay Stadium
topPlaces.description: Jawaharlal Navoday Vidhyalay Stadium is a cricket ground near Una, Himachal Pradesh, India. The first recorded match held on the ground came in January 1999 when Bengal Under-16s played Madhya Pradesh Under-16s.

topPlaces.name: Paramount Cricket Promotion Association Stadium
topPlaces.description: PCPA Stadium (Paramount Cricket Parmotion Association Stadium) is a cricket stadium located in the city of Santokhgarh in Himachal Pradesh. The stadium is home to Punjab, Himachal cricket teams matches and Cricket Coaching.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (75 km) and the nearest railway station is Ludhiana Junction (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nangal Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nangal, Mehatpur Basdehra, Dehlan, Himachal Pradesh, Santokhgarh, Bhakra Dam.
